title: OnePlus officially exits Europe and North America, will continue in India
description: OnePlus announced today that it will be exiting the European and North American markets. In a separate announcement, the company confirmed that its India operations will continue. For European and North American markets, the company will no longer be releasing new products. However, existing devices will continue to receive software updates and security patches as per their schedule. Customer service channels will also remain open to honor warranty and support claims. The North American Community website and app will shut down on August 16, 2026 at 11:59PM ET. However, European...

title: Infinix Hot 70 Pro unboxing
description: We've got an exclusive first look at the new Infinix Hot 70 Pro for you guys, plus an unboxing. Let's get to it. The phone ships with a packed box. You get a 45W charger, a USB-A to USB-C cable, and a nice case with a pretty accented outline. Unboxing the Infinix Hot 70 Pro Ours is the Mirage Green model, which has a grippy vegan leather finish on the rear panel. The phone feels balanced in the hand. What's special about the Mirage Green model is the special light-up effect that's fused into the vegan leather. Hit the panel with some UV light, and these small light-up...

title: Realme India confirms its future devices will launch with ColorOS 17
description: Earlier this month, a media report claimed that Realme UI will be merged with Oppo's ColorOS to help reduce R&D costs. We had a meeting with Realme India, and the brand confirmed that going forward, Realme smartphones will indeed launch with ColorOS. Well, ColorOS 17 to be specific. Realme said this move is aimed at streamlining things and simplifying the update process. While the Oppo sub-brand didn't tell us which Realme smartphone will be the first to launch with ColorOS 17, it's safe to say that we'll see ColorOS 17 on Oppo smartphones first. Realme also confirmed that ColorOS...

title: Infinix Hot 70 Pro is official, comes in six different finishes
description: The Infinix Hot 70 Pro is here, and just like its non-Pro sibling, it's got some fancy finishes. Three of the special ones include Thermo Orange, a finish that darkens or lightens with heat and cold; Mirage Green, which lights up like a starry sky at night under UV light; and Silk Glow Purple, which has a dynamic shine with embedded nanocrystal texture in the back panel. Then there's Depth Ring White, which is also one of the special ones with a 3D effect. And finally, two more conventional finishes complete the tally - Night Pulse (black) and Dive Blue. All variants weigh 205...

title: Redmi 17 4G renders and specifications leak
description: Earlier this month, details about the global variant of the Redmi 17 5G surfaced online. Now, a fresh leak has revealed the design, color options, and complete specifications of the Redmi 17 4G. According to the leak, the Redmi 17 4G will be offered in Black, Deep Blue, Lotus Purple, and Oak Green color options. The smartphone is said to weigh 232g and measure 8.8mm thick. It will reportedly be powered by the MediaTek Helio G91 Ultra chipset and will be available in 4GB/128GB and 6GB/256GB configurations. The handset is also said to ship with Android 16-based HyperOS 3. The Redmi...

title: Redmi Note 17 Pro teardown video shows off the phone's internals
description: Redmi unveiled the Note 17 Pro alongside the Note 17 yesterday for the Chinese market. Now, there's already a full disassembly video of the Note 17 Pro out, courtesy of Chinese channel WekiHome. It's obviously in Chinese, but YouTube's built-in closed captions are very good at translating what the presenter is saying in our experience, so make sure you have them turned on. As it happens with many smartphones, getting inside the Redmi Note 17 Pro is a matter of first removing the back cover, which here is made of plastic. The inside of the back cover has a lot of foam to protect from water...

title: Another report confirms OnePlus' imminent demise, reveals it's going to exit India too
description: A couple of days ago a usually reliable source of smartphone-related information claimed that OnePlus will be leaving the US and Europe as soon as this week. Now a new report from a different reliable source, Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, confirms the brand's imminent exit from the aforementioned key markets. But this one adds more details. In 2027, OnePlus will apparently also withdraw from all other markets except China - thus including India, where it's recently launched the N6 and started teasing a new model in the line earlier today. OnePlus 15 Restructuring is also coming for...

title: Google Pixel 11 teaser video confirms Pixel Glow feature, pre-order date
description: Last week, Google announced that it will be unveiling the Pixel 11 family at an event in New York City on August 12. Now, the company has uploaded a very short teaser video for the devices onto the homepage of its online store of all places. Here it is for your viewing pleasure. The video shows a much-rumored upcoming feature called Pixel Glow, which is basically a circular LED light that can show a full range of colors. This will be used as a glorified notification light, of course. With its full-color array, hopefully it will be customizable to show different colors for...

title: More details about OpenAI's smart speaker surface
description: OpenAI's first device will be a screen-less smart speaker, according to a new report from Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. It will be designed to be used inside the home but will be easy to move around the house and will use a rechargeable battery. Like any other smart speaker, it will be able to control smart home appliances, play media, answer questions, respond to messages, and of course this being an OpenAI product, it will have ChatGPT built-in. The chatbot will use a more advanced version of the new ChatGPT voice mode, with which you can have a true back-and-forth conversation, interrupting...

title: Huawei MatePad Air (2026) hands-on
description: During its yesterday's event in Malaysia, Huawei introduced the Pura 90s series along with the FreeClip 2 S and MatePad Air (2026). We had our chance to meet the devices in person, so here's a quick rundown of the updated MatePad Air for this year. MatePad Air (2026) Just like its predecessor, the MatePad Air (2026) features a 12-inch display, but instead of a plain old IPS LCD panel, the newer Air swaps it out for a brighter OLED one with a 144Hz refresh rate. What's more, the screen features Huawei's PaperMatte coating that makes it look like paper. Reflections are reduced,...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra full specs leak
description: We recently saw leaked renders showing Samsung's upcoming Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, and now it's time for their full specs. These have been leaked today by a usually reliable source, so let's dive right in. As you may already know, the Galaxy Z Fold8 will be the wide-style foldable that's aiming to compete with the iPhone Ultra. This will have a 7.6-inch 2448x1848 main folding screen with a 4:3 aspect ratio, and a 5.5-inch cover display with 1248x1972 resolution. Both panels will have 120Hz refresh rate. The display substrate is titanium now, which should make the crease...

title: Third-party app stores will soon be available through the Google Play Store in the US
description: Running an app store is a profitable business – one that platform makers are keen to protect. That used to be the case with Google too, but the conclusion of a 6-year legal battle with Epic Games has come to a surprise ending that will make third-party app stores that much more accessible. The story started in 2020 when Epic sued Google, alleging that Google held a monopoly in the Android app distribution market and in-app billing services. A jury sided with Epic, but this then went through an appeal process until, eventually, the two companies reached a settlement agreement in...

title: Samsung's best pre-order perk won't be available for the Galaxy Z Fold8, Fold8 Ultra, and Flip8
description: The best part about pre-ordering a new Samsung smartphone has, for the past few years, been that you can get a 'free' storage upgrade. Pay for one storage tier, get the higher one for no additional cost. However, a new report from Korea today claims Samsung will not be offering this perk for the upcoming Galaxy Z Fold8, Fold8 Ultra, and Flip8, which it will unveil on July 22. Instead, the company will only cover 50% of the price difference between the 256GB option and the 512GB model. So basically, you'll pay half of the difference to get the higher storage tier when you...

title: Carrier-financed iPhones in the US now get locked to that carrier
description: It used to be that all iPhones bought directly from Apple in the US came unlocked, meaning that you could use them with any carrier – even if you bought the phone with financing from another carrier. Now that’s no longer the case. Apple has updated its FAQ and it now says that iPhones bought with carrier financing are locked to that carrier until the phone is paid off. Once you complete the final payment, the phone will be unlocked and you will be free to switch to a different carrier. The updated FAQ states that you no longer get an unlocked phone with carrier financing This...

title: Apple Intelligence finally gets the green light from Chinese authorities
description: While you may very well have been underwhelmed by Apple Intelligence, at least you had access to it. That hasn't been the case for those in China so far, even as we are now 21 months after the initial launch. However, that is finally about to change. Apple Intelligence has now received the green light from the Chinese government. The service has been registered with the Chinese cyberspace regulator for use on iPhones in the country. All AI products need to undergo this step before they can be offered in China. In China, Apple Intelligence will allegedly "incorporate capabilities...

title: Nvidia GeForce Now is available in India with free, Performance and Ultimate tiers
description: We’re surprised it took this long, but the timing makes a lot of sense given how expensive gaming hardware has gotten. GeForce Now, Nvidia’s game streaming service, is now available in India. The service first entered the country with an open beta in April, but that only offered 90-day passes. Now, it’s widely available and it offers monthly passes instead. The free ad-supported tier gets you 1080p @ 60fps streaming with gaming sessions limited to 1 hour. This is a great way to test things out (especially your Internet connection) and might work for games that are played in short...

title: Study finds that Google's AI features create "unacceptable risks" for children
description: Multiple Internet giants have found themselves under the scrutiny of lawmakers, regulators and organizations concerned with the safety of children on the web. Google and its AI Search are the latest to draw criticism. Interestingly, it may have been the success of the Chromebooks that caused this – they are very popular as school laptops. The Common Sense Media organization explains that the thing that separates Google’s AI from all the other AI services is that it is “ubiquitous on children's personal and school-issued devices.” Who needs to learn math when AI can just solve the...

title: Realme Narzo 100x arrives with a 144Hz screen and an 8,000 mAh battery
description: Realme today expanded its portfolio of Narzo Series smartphones with the unveiling of the Narzo 100x. The biggest highlight of the Realme Narzo 100x is its 8,000 mAh Titan battery, advertised to deliver up to three days of battery life on a single charge. The 8.8mm thick smartphone supports 45W charging, all-scenario bypass charging, and reverse wired charging. The Realme Narzo 100x is built around a 6.81" HD+ LCD with a 144Hz refresh rate and 1,200 nits peak brightness. Realme says the smartphone will offer consistent 60FPS smoothness in BGMI, MLBB, and Free Fire, but hasn't...

title: Google Images turns 25, introduces a new Pinterest-like feature
description: Google Images launched 25 years ago. Back then it was as simple as it gets – type in some words and get matching images as a result. The image search feature has grown considerably more capable over the intervening years and is now getting a major overhaul to celebrate its quarter century anniversary. Google Images is getting a new home page. You may not see anything different yet – it will roll out over the coming weeks starting with the desktop page in US English. But once it goes live, you will see something that looks and works kind of like Pinterest. The new Collections...

title: New OnePlus N6 series model teased to launch soon
description: The OnePlus N6 has barely been on the market for a month, but the company is already preparing to expand the lineup. OnePlus has confirmed that a new N6 series smartphone, the OnePlus N6x, will be launching soon. Amazon India has already put up a dedicated microsite for the OnePlus N6x, although it doesn't reveal any specifications or availability details just yet. Meanwhile, a tipster claims that the upcoming smartphone will feature familiar hardware, suggesting it could share many of its specifications with the recently launched OnePlus N6. OnePlus N6 The OnePlus N6...
